sections.name: Consent Management
questions: Does your financial institution obtain explicit consent before collecting personal data from customers?
Are your consent mechanisms for financial products presented in clear, jargon-free language?
Do you have separate consent mechanisms for different financial services and products?
Can customers easily withdraw their consent without affecting essential banking services?
options: Yes, with clear affirmative action required
Partially, but consent mechanisms need improvement
No, we rely on implied consent
Yes, we use simple language and avoid financial jargon
No, our consent notices use technical/legal terminology
Yes, we obtain separate consent for each financial product/service
Partially, but some consents are bundled
No, we use a single consent for all services
Yes, through a simple, accessible process
Yes, but the process is somewhat complicated

sections.name: Purpose Limitation
questions: Does your financial institution clearly document all purposes for which customer financial data is processed?
Is customer financial data used only for the specific purposes for which it was collected?
Do you have mechanisms to prevent customer financial data use beyond stated purposes, especially for marketing?
options: Yes, all purposes are documented and reviewed regularly
No, purposes are not systematically documented
Yes, strictly limited to stated purposes
No, data is used for additional purposes
Yes, we have technical and policy controls

sections.name: Data Minimization
questions: Does your financial institution collect only financial data that is necessary for specified purposes?
Do you have processes to identify and remove redundant customer financial data after KYC verification?
Do you limit collection of sensitive financial information to only what is required by regulations?
options: Yes, we regularly review and minimize data collection
Partially, some unnecessary data may be collected
Yes, only required sensitive data is collected
Partially, some excess sensitive data is collected
No, we collect extensive sensitive data

sections.name: Data Retention
questions: Does your financial institution have data retention policies that comply with both DPDP and financial regulations?
Are retention periods defined for different categories of financial customer data?
Do you have automated processes to delete or anonymize customer data after retention periods expire, while meeting RBI requirements?
options: Yes, comprehensive policies balancing both requirements
Yes, specific periods for each data category
Yes, fully automated within regulatory constraints

sections.name: Data Subject Rights
questions: Does your financial institution have procedures to handle customer data access requests?
Can you provide customers with copies of their financial data in a structured, portable format?
Do you have processes to correct inaccurate customer financial data upon request?
Can you erase customer personal data upon request while preserving mandatory financial records?
options: Yes, with separation of regulatory/optional data

sections.name: Security Measures
questions: Does your financial institution implement industry-standard security measures for personal financial data?
Do you conduct regular security assessments of banking systems processing personal data?
Do you have role-based access controls limiting who can access customer financial data?
Is customer financial data encrypted at rest and in transit using banking-grade encryption?
options: Yes, following RBI and financial industry standards
Yes, strict role-based access with principle of least privilege

sections.name: Data Breach Management
questions: Does your financial institution have a data breach response plan specific to financial data?
Can you detect financial data breaches in a timely manner with advanced monitoring?
Do you have procedures to notify authorities and RBI of data breaches within required timeframes?
Do you document all data breaches affecting financial customer data and remediation actions?
options: Yes, comprehensive and tested plan for financial context
Yes, advanced monitoring systems in place
Yes, clear procedures for all required authorities

sections.name: Cross-Border Data Transfers
questions: Does your financial institution transfer customer financial data outside India?
If yes, do you ensure adequate protection for cross-border financial data transfers?
Do you maintain detailed records of all cross-border financial data transfers as required by RBI?
options: Yes, detailed records as per RBI requirements

sections.name: Financial-Specific Compliance
questions: Does your institution implement DPDP compliance that aligns with PMLA requirements for KYC data?
Is credit scoring and automated financial decision-making explainable to customers?
Do you have special safeguards for sensitive financial information like income, loan history, and investment data?
Are third-party financial service providers contractually bound to your DPDP compliance standards?

Consent Management.Partially, but consent mechanisms need i: Implement clearer financial consent mechanisms with explicit opt-in options specific to banking products
Consent Management.No, we rely on implied consent: Replace implied consent with explicit consent mechanisms for all financial services
Consent Management.Partially, but needs simplification: Simplify financial consent language and remove banking jargon
Consent Management.No, our consent notices use technical/le: Rewrite financial consent notices in plain language, avoiding banking and legal jargon
Consent Management.Partially, but some consents are bundled: Separate consent for different financial products and services
Consent Management.No, we use a single consent for all serv: Implement granular consent options for different financial products and services
Consent Management.Yes, but the process is somewhat complic: Simplify the consent withdrawal process for banking customers
Consent Management.No, withdrawal affects essential service: Restructure consent system to allow withdrawal without affecting essential banking services

Purpose Limitation.Partially, some purposes are documented: Document all purposes for financial data processing in your banking policies
Purpose Limitation.No, purposes are not systematically docu: Create a comprehensive financial data processing register
Purpose Limitation.Mostly, with rare exceptions: Implement stricter controls to prevent financial data repurposing
Purpose Limitation.No, data is used for additional purposes: Establish clear purpose limitation policy for customer financial data
Purpose Limitation.Partially, some controls exist: Strengthen controls to prevent financial data use beyond stated purposes
Purpose Limitation.No, limited controls exist: Implement technical and policy controls for financial data purpose limitation

Data Minimization.Partially, some unnecessary data may be: Review financial data collection processes to minimize data collected
Data Minimization.No, we collect excess data: Conduct a financial data inventory and eliminate unnecessary collection
Data Minimization.Partially, occasional reviews: Implement regular financial data minimization reviews
Data Minimization.No systematic process: Establish a systematic process for identifying redundant financial data
Data Minimization.Partially, some excess sensitive data is: Review and minimize collection of sensitive financial information
Data Minimization.No, we collect extensive sensitive data: Limit collection of sensitive financial data to regulatory requirements only

Data Retention.Partially, basic policy exists: Develop a comprehensive retention policy that balances DPDP with financial regulations
Data Retention.No documented policy: Create a formal retention policy aligned with both DPDP and financial regulations
Data Retention.Partially, general periods defined: Define specific retention periods for each category of financial data
Data Retention.No defined periods: Establish clear retention periods for all financial data categories
Data Retention.Partially automated with manual oversigh: Enhance automation of financial data deletion processes
Data Retention.Manual process only: Implement semi-automated financial data deletion processes
Data Retention.No systematic process: Establish a systematic process for financial data deletion after required retention periods

Data Subject Rights.Basic procedures exist: Enhance procedures for handling financial data access requests
Data Subject Rights.No formal procedures: Establish formal procedures for financial data subject access requests
Data Subject Rights.Yes, but manual process: Develop more automated export functionality for financial data
Data Subject Rights.Limited capability: Improve capability to provide financial data in structured formats
Data Subject Rights.No capability: Implement systems to export financial data in structured formats
Data Subject Rights.Basic process exists: Enhance processes for correcting inaccurate financial data
Data Subject Rights.Ad hoc handling: Formalize processes for handling financial data correction requests
Data Subject Rights.No process: Establish processes for correcting financial data upon request
Data Subject Rights.Partially, in primary systems: Extend financial data deletion capabilities while preserving mandatory records
Data Subject Rights.Limited capability: Improve financial data erasure capabilities across systems
Data Subject Rights.No capability: Implement mechanisms for erasure that separate regulatory and optional financial data

Security Measures.Basic security measures: Enhance security measures to meet financial industry standards
Security Measures.Minimal security measures: Implement comprehensive security based on RBI and financial sector requirements
Security Measures.Occasional assessments: Establish regular security assessment schedule for financial systems
Security Measures.Rarely or never: Implement regular security assessments of financial data processing systems
Security Measures.Basic access controls: Implement role-based access with least privilege for financial data access
Security Measures.Limited or no access controls: Develop comprehensive access control framework for financial data
Security Measures.Partial encryption: Implement full banking-grade encryption for financial data at rest and in transit
Security Measures.Limited or no encryption: Establish strong encryption standards for all financial data

Data Breach Management.Basic plan exists: Develop and test a comprehensive breach response plan for financial data
Data Breach Management.No formal plan: Create and implement a formal data breach response plan for financial data
Data Breach Management.Basic detection capabilities: Enhance breach detection systems for financial data
Data Breach Management.Limited or no detection capabilities: Implement advanced monitoring systems for financial data breach detection
Data Breach Management.Basic procedures exist: Enhance procedures for notifying authorities including RBI
Data Breach Management.No formal procedures: Establish procedures for timely breach notifications to all required authorities
Data Breach Management.Basic documentation: Improve documentation processes for financial data breaches

Cross-Border Data Transfers.Yes, to RBI-approved countries only: Review adequacy decisions and transfer mechanisms for financial data
Cross-Border Data Transfers.Yes, including to non-approved countries: Implement appropriate safeguards for all international financial data transfers
Cross-Border Data Transfers.Basic measures: Enhance protection measures for cross-border financial data transfers
Cross-Border Data Transfers.Limited or no measures: Implement adequate RBI-approved safeguards for international transfers
Cross-Border Data Transfers.Basic records: Improve record-keeping for cross-border financial data transfers
Cross-Border Data Transfers.Limited or no records: Establish comprehensive records of all financial data transfers as per RBI requirements

Financial-Specific Compliance.Partially integrated: Develop an integrated compliance framework that simultaneously satisfies DPDP and financial regulations
Financial-Specific Compliance.No, separate compliance tracks: Consolidate DPDP and PMLA/KYC compliance processes to ensure consistency
Financial-Specific Compliance.Partially explainable: Improve explanation mechanisms for credit decisions and automated financial processes
Financial-Specific Compliance.No, black-box decisions: Implement explainable AI/algorithms for all automated financial decisions
Financial-Specific Compliance.Basic safeguards: Enhance safeguards for sensitive financial information with additional encryption and access controls
Financial-Specific Compliance.Limited safeguards: Implement comprehensive protections for sensitive financial data
Financial-Specific Compliance.Basic requirements: Strengthen third-party contractual requirements for financial data protection
Financial-Specific Compliance.No specific requirements: Develop and implement detailed DPDP compliance requirements for all financial service partners
